# WI-QUIC-API-0006 - Incursa.Quic Public Loopback Connection Comparison Work Item

## Summary

Add a bounded BenchmarkDotNet suite that compares the Incursa and `System.Net.Quic` public loopback connection facades at the same abstraction level.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-API-0015

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-API-0006

## Planned Changes

- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite that runs matched loopback public-facade connection-establishment workloads against Incursa.Quic and `System.Net.Quic`.
- Keep the workload set limited to listener creation, connection accept and connect, and connection disposal on the same local machine.
- Share certificate generation, client trust setup, and loopback endpoint allocation between both stack-specific paths so the comparison stays aligned.
- Omit unsupported implementations via each stack's own `IsSupported` marker instead of synthesizing placeholder benchmark results.
- Update the benchmark documentation and the owning public API trace artifacts so this connection-only comparison boundary is explicit and does not overlap the internal helper baselines or overclaim public stream-transfer support.

## Out of Scope

- Comparing internal helper, parser, recovery, or congestion benchmarks to public `System.Net.Quic` workloads.
- HTTP/3 or interop-runner performance claims.
- Public stream-transfer or throughput comparison.
- Public API shape changes.
- Broader transfer, retry, 0-RTT, or key-update support.

## Verification Plan

Run the focused public API loopback requirement-home guards for listener/connect establishment and stream-entry regression safety, then run the new public loopback benchmark suite with BenchmarkDotNet `Dry` and `Short` if the harness is stable, and preserve the exported reports. Finish with repo-local SpecTrace render/check, `git diff --check`, and the known-baseline SpecTrace validation profile.

## Completion Notes

Completed the permanent public-facade loopback connection benchmark suite and preserved Dry plus Short evidence under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/`. The current proven floor compares only connect/accept/dispose on the same machine and leaves public stream-transfer comparison out of scope until that workload has its own traced proof boundary.
